quran.text: The example of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is like a seed [of grain] which grows seven spikes; in each spike is a hundred grains. And Allah multiplies [His reward] for whom He wills. And Allah is all-Encompassing and Knowing.
And spend in the way of Allah and do not throw [yourselves] with your [own] hands into destruction [by refraining]. And do good; indeed, Allah loves the doers of good.
Never will you attain the good [reward] until you spend [in the way of Allah] from that which you love. And whatever you spend - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.
And they give food in spite of love for it to the needy, the orphan, and the captive, [Saying], 'We feed you only for the countenance of Allah. We wish not from you reward or gratitude.'
And establish prayer and give zakah, and whatever good you put forward for yourselves - you will find it with Allah. Indeed, Allah of what you do, is Seeing.
So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it.
And in their wealth there is a right for the petitioner and the deprived.
So as for the orphan, do not oppress [him]. And as for the petitioner, do not repel [him].
Those who spend their wealth in the night and in the day, secretly and publicly - they will have their reward with their Lord. And no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.
Satan threatens you with poverty and orders you to immorality, while Allah promises you forgiveness from Him and bounty. And Allah is all-Encompassing and Knowing.
And whatever you spend of anything, He will replace it. And He is the best of providers.

Take, [O, Muhammad], from their wealth a charity by which you purify them and cause them increase, and invoke [Allah's blessings] upon them. Indeed, your invocations are reassurance for them. And Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

Righteousness is not that you turn your faces toward the east or the west, but [true] righteousness is [in] one who believes in Allah... and gives wealth, in spite of love for it, to relatives, orphans, the needy, the traveler, those who ask [for help], and for freeing slaves.
And do not make your hand [as] chained to your neck or extend it completely and [thereby] become blamed and insolvent.
Have you seen the one who denies the final judgment? That is the one who repulses the orphan, and does not encourage the feeding of the poor.
Indeed, the men who practice charity and the women who practice charity and [they who] have loaned Allah a goodly loan - it will be multiplied for them, and they will have a noble reward.

If you disclose your charitable expenditures, they are good; but if you conceal them and give them to the poor, it is better for you, and He will remove from you some of your misdeeds. And Allah, with what you do, is [fully] Acquainted.
And cooperate in righteousness and piety, but do not cooperate in sin and aggression.
So fear Allah as much as you are able, and listen and obey and spend; it is better for your souls. And whoever is protected from the stinginess of his soul - it is those who will be the successful.
And what will make you know what is the difficult path? It is the freeing of a slave, or feeding on a day of severe hunger an orphan of near relationship or a needy person in misery.
O you who have believed, spend from the good things which you have earned and from that which We have produced for you from the earth.
And what you give in zakah, desiring the countenance of Allah - those are the multipliers.

But the righteous one will avoid it - [He] who gives his wealth to purify himself, And has in his mind no favor from anyone for which a reward is expected, But only seeking the countenance of his Lord, Most High. And he is going to be satisfied.
And give the relative his right, and the needy, and the traveler, and do not spend wastefully.
Allah will deprive usury of all blessing, but will give increase for deeds of charity.
And seek with that which Allah has given you the home of the Hereafter, and do not forget your share of the world. And do good as Allah has done good to you.
My mercy encompasses all things. So I will decree it for those who are righteous and give zakah and those who believe in Our signs.

Indeed, mankind was created anxious: When evil touches him, impatient, and when good touches him, withholding [of it]. Except the observers of prayer... And those in whose wealth there is a recognized right, for the petitioner and the deprived.

They ask you, [O Muhammad], what they should spend. Say, 'Whatever you spend of good is [to be] for parents and relatives and orphans and the needy and the traveler. And whatever you do of good - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.'
Believe in Allah and His Messenger and spend out of that of which He has made you successors. For those who have believed among you and spent, for them is a great reward.
And whatever you spend in the cause of Allah will be fully repaid to you, and you will not be wronged.

Those who spend their wealth [in Allah's way] by night and by day, secretly and publicly - they will have their reward with their Lord. And no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.

And they are those who are steadfast in seeking the pleasure of their Lord, and they establish regular prayer, and they spend of what We have provided for them, secretly and openly, and they repel evil with good. For such people, there is the ultimate abode.

And let not those of virtue among you and wealth swear not to give [aid] to their relatives and the needy and the emigrants for the cause of Allah, and let them pardon and overlook. Would you not like that Allah should forgive you? And Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us.
The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months.
So let them worship the Lord of this House, Who has fed them, [saving them] from hunger and made them safe, [saving them] from fear.

Charity is for the poor who are restricted for the cause of Allah, unable to move about in the land. An ignorant [person] would think them rich because of their restraint. You will know them by their mark. They do not ask people persistently. And whatever you spend of good - indeed, Allah is Knowing of it.
So when you have finished [your duties], then stand up [for worship]. And to your Lord direct [your] longing.
And [he was] a believer in Allah, the Most Great, and he did not encourage the feeding of the poor.
Indeed, he who purifies himself (by giving zakat and charity) shall achieve success.
The life of this world is only the enjoyment of deception.
Woe to those who pray, but are heedless of their prayer; those who make a show of piety, but refuse to give even the smallest help to others.
And establish prayer and give zakah and obey the Messenger that you may receive mercy.
He who is protected from the avarice of his own soul, it is they who will be the successful.
For them who have done good is the best [reward] and even more.
And there is no creature on earth but that upon Allah is its provision, and He knows its place of dwelling and place of storage. All is in a clear register.
Indeed, my Lord extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]. But most of the people do not know.
Is there any reward for good other than good?
And be patient, for indeed, Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
And those who spend [in charity] out of what We have provided them.
For those who give in charity, men and women, and loan to Allah a Beautiful Loan, it shall be increased manifold (to their credit), and they shall have (besides) a liberal reward.

hadith.text: The Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was the most generous of people, and he was the most generous during Ramadan when Jibril met him. Jibril used to meet him every night in Ramadan to study the Qur'an, and the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) was more generous with good deeds than the blowing wind.

Whoever provides the food for a fasting person to break his fast with, then for him is the same reward as his (the fasting person's), without anything being diminished from the reward of the fasting person.
When a man dies, his deeds come to an end except for three things: Sadaqah Jariyah (ceaseless charity); a knowledge which is beneficial, or a virtuous descendant who prays for him.
Protect yourself from hell-fire even by giving a piece of a date as charity.
Charity does not decrease wealth, no one forgives another except that Allah increases his honor, and no one humbles himself for the sake of Allah except that Allah raises his status.
The believer's shade on the Day of Resurrection will be his charity.

A charity is due for every joint in each person on every day the sun comes up: to act justly between two people is a charity; to help a man with his mount, lifting him onto it or hoisting up his belongings onto it, is a charity...
The upper hand is better than the lower hand. The upper hand is the one that gives, and the lower hand is the one that receives.
I and the person who looks after an orphan and provides for him, will be in Paradise like this,' putting his index and middle fingers together.
Every act of goodness is a charity.
Give charity without delay, for it stands in the way of calamity.
The best charity is to give water to drink.
He is not a believer who eats his fill while his neighbor beside him is hungry.
The best of people are those who are most beneficial to people.
A man's true wealth is the good he does in this world.
The one who looks after a widow and a poor person is like a Mujahid (warrior) in the cause of Allah, or like the one who fasts all the day and stands in prayer all the night.
There is a reward for serving any living being.
Your smiling in the face of your brother is a charity.
Allah is in the aid of His servant as long as His servant is in the aid of his brother.
Whoever relieves a believer’s distress of the distressful aspects of this world, Allah will rescue him from a difficulty of the difficulties of the Hereafter.
A man whom Allah has given wealth and he spends it in the right way...
O son of Adam, spend, and I will spend on you.
The charity of a Muslim cools the heat of the grave for him, and the believer will be in the shade of his charity on the Day of Resurrection.
The one who strives to sponsor a widow and a needy person is like the one who strives in the cause of Allah.
A man's spending on his family is a charity.
Do not withhold your money, lest Allah withholds from you.
Every Muslim has to give in charity... He should work with his hands and benefit himself and also give in charity.
The best charity is that which is practiced by a wealthy person. And start giving first to your dependents.
If anyone of you is pleased to be safe from the great distress of the Day of Resurrection, he should grant respite to the one in straitened circumstances or remit his debt.
A man giving a dirham in his lifetime is better for him than giving one hundred dirhams at the time of his death.
A kind word is a form of charity.
Whoever plants a tree and a human being or an animal eats from it, it is a charity for him.
The parable of the believers in their affection, mercy, and compassion for each other is that of a body. When any limb aches, the whole body reacts with sleeplessness and fever.
He who sleeps on a full stomach whilst his neighbour is hungry is not one of us.
Allah will not be merciful to those who are not merciful to mankind.
The most beloved of deeds to Allah are those that are most consistent, even if it is small.
Whoever builds a mosque for Allah, Allah will build for him a house like it in Paradise.
The wealth of a man will not diminish by giving charity.
Fear Allah and be just among your children.
Charity extinguishes sin as water extinguishes fire.
The most beloved of places to Allah are the mosques, and the most hated of places to Allah are the marketplaces.
Do not consider any act of kindness insignificant, even if it is meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
There is no person who gives charity from his good earnings - and Allah only accepts that which is good - but the Merciful takes it with His right hand.
The people of mercy will be shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Be merciful to those on the earth and the One in the heavens will have mercy upon you.
Whoever seeks a path to knowledge, Allah will make his path to Paradise easy.
The best among you is the one who is best to his family.
He who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of pride shall not enter Paradise.
Whoever introduces a good practice in Islam, he will have its reward and the reward of those who act upon it after him without anything being diminished from their rewards.
Zakah is a bridge for Islam.
The best of you are those who are best in paying off their debts.
A Muslim is a brother of another Muslim, so he should not oppress him, nor should he hand him over to an oppressor.
When the month of Ramadan starts, the gates of heaven are opened and the gates of Hell are closed and the devils are chained.
Take on only as much as you can do of good deeds, for the best of deeds is that which is done consistently, even if it is little.
Whoever among you sees an evil, let him change it with his hand; if he is unable, then with his tongue; and if he is unable, then with his heart; and that is the weakest of faith.
Verily, actions are but by intentions, and every man shall have only that which he intended.

scholars.text: Do not feel ashamed if the amount of charity is small, because to refuse the needy is an act of greater shame.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha, Saying 67

scholars.text: The sign that Allah is pleased with a servant is that He makes him a source of meeting the needs of people.
The richest of the rich is the one who is not a prisoner of greed.
scholars.source: Ali ibn Abi Talib (R.A), Nahj al-Balagha, Saying 371

scholars.text: If you are unable to do a good deed, then restrain yourself from doing a bad deed. That, too, is a charity.
The cure for five things is five things: The cure for a hard heart is to stroke the head of an orphan, to show kindness to him, and to give him some of what you have.
A man's true wealth is the good he does in the world. As for his worldly belongings, they are for his heirs.
Be like a flower that gives its fragrance even to the hand that crushes it.
The real dunya (worldly life) is the one you spend in the way of Allah.
The less you have, the more you give.
Three things cannot be retrieved: the arrow once sped from the bow, the word spoken, and the opportunity lost. Spend in charity before the opportunity is lost.
Your rizq (provision) is written. What is meant for you will reach you even if it is between two mountains. What is not meant for you will not reach you even if it is between your two lips.
Sincerity is that your private deeds are better than your public ones.
I was not pleased with a single dirham I spent on myself, but I was pleased with a single dirham I spent on a needy person.
The fruit of love is to benefit others. The fruit of knowledge is to benefit oneself. The fruit of charity is to benefit both.
He who has a thousand friends has not a friend to spare, and he who has one enemy will meet him everywhere. Be charitable and you will find friends.
If you see a person who has been given more than you in money and beauty, then look at those who have been given less.
The best jihad is the one against your own desires, including the desire to hoard wealth.
Knowledge without action is vanity, and action without knowledge is insanity. Give from your knowledge and your wealth.
The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever. Spend from your 'prison' for your paradise.
Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
The greatest of people is he who is most self-sufficient from the people.

The heart gets sick, and its cure is in turning to Allah. It gets thirsty, and its drink is in remembering Allah. It gets naked, and its adornment is in fearing Allah. And it gets hungry, and its food is in loving Allah.
A person’s true worth is what he has given in the way of Allah.

The miser is far from Allah, far from Paradise, far from the people, and close to the Hellfire. The generous person is close to Allah, close to Paradise, close to the people, and far from the Hellfire.
To be of service to others is to be of service to God.
The tears that fall for the sake of Allah are pearls that are too precious to be seen by anyone but Him.
The one who is truly poor is not the one who has no money, but the one who has no good deeds.
The deed that is most beloved to Allah is the one that is done regularly, even if it is small.
If you knew the power of a single good deed, you would not leave a single day without doing one.
The one who guides to good will have a reward like the one who does it.
The most complete of believers in faith are those with the best character. And the best of you are those who are best to their women.
The dunya is a distraction from Allah. The more you take from it, the more you are distracted.
The best of you are those who are of the most benefit to others.
Charity is a proof of one's faith.
The one who helps a widow and a poor person is like a warrior in the cause of Allah.
Whoever is not grateful to people is not grateful to Allah.
The best investment is in the Hereafter.
When you give, give with a cheerful heart.
The hand that gives is above the hand that takes.
Allah is more pleased with the repentance of His servant than a person who finds his lost camel in the desert.
The world is three days: as for yesterday, it has vanished; as for tomorrow, you may not reach it; but as for today, it is yours, so work on it.
When Allah loves a servant, He tests him.
The tongue is like a lion. If you let it loose, it will wound someone.
A wise man's day is divided into four parts: one for supplicating his Lord, one for taking account of himself, one for reflecting on the creation of Allah, and one for his worldly needs.
The path to guidance is through knowledge, not through ignorance. Seek knowledge to guide your charity.
The true measure of a man is not how he behaves in moments of comfort and convenience, but how he stands at times of challenge and controversy.
The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.
No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
To give up something you love for the sake of Allah is the highest form of worship.
The beginning of knowledge is the fear of Allah, and the beginning of wealth is contentment.
Ramadan is a month to fast, pray, read Quran, and give charity. And to forgive and forget.
